heading: War memorials not built by the United States Government
label: 2106 War memorials not built by the United States Government

L. 104–275, title VI, § 602(a)110 Stat. 3344Jan. 2, 1974Oct. 9, 19962106(b)(2)36:125(b)(1) (last sentence), (d).2106(b)(3)36:125(b)(2)(A), (d).2106(c)36:125(b)(2)(B), (d).2106(d)36:125(b)(3).2106(e)36:125(c), (d).In subsections (b), (c), and (e), the text of 36:125(d) is omitted as unnecessary.In subsection (b)(1), before clause (A), the words “in its discretion” and “before, on, or after the effective date of this subsection” are omitted as unnecessary. Maintenance and Repair of Pacific Battle Monuments, , , provided that:Pub. L. 103–160, div. A, title III, § 369107 Stat. 1634Nov. 30, 1993“(a).—AuthorityThe Commandant of the Marine Corps may provide necessary minor maintenance and repairs to the Pacific battle monuments until such time as the Secretary of the American Battle Monuments Commission and the Commandant of the Marine Corps agree that the repair and maintenance will be performed by the American Battle Monuments Commission.“(b).—FundingOf the amounts authorized to be appropriated to the Marine Corps for operation and maintenance in a fiscal year, not more than $15,000 may be made available to repair and maintain Pacific battle monuments, except that of the amounts available to the Marine Corps for operation and maintenance in fiscal year 1994, $150,000 may be made available to repair and relocate a monument located on Iwo Jima commemorating the heroic efforts of United States military personnel during World War II.”

A, title III, § 369107 Stat. 1634Nov. 30, 1993“(a).—AuthorityThe Commandant of the Marine Corps may provide necessary minor maintenance and repairs to the Pacific battle monuments until such time as the Secretary of the American Battle Monuments Commission and the Commandant of the Marine Corps agree that the repair and maintenance will be performed by the American Battle Monuments Commission.“(b).—FundingOf the amounts authorized to be appropriated to the Marine Corps for operation and maintenance in a fiscal year, not more than $15,000 may be made available to repair and maintain Pacific battle monuments, except that of the amounts available to the Marine Corps for operation and maintenance in fiscal year 1994, $150,000 may be made available to repair and relocate a monument located on Iwo Jima commemorating the heroic efforts of United States military personnel during World War II.”

children.heading: .—Cooperation With Others
children.label: a .—Cooperation With Others
children.text: The American Battle Monuments Commission may cooperate with citizens of the United States, States, municipalities, or associations desiring to build war memorials outside the continental limits of the United States in the way the Commission decides. An administrative agency of the United States Government may give assistance to build the memorial only if a plan for the memorial has been approved under this chapter.

children.heading: .—Control, Administration, and Maintenance of War Memorials
children.label: b .—Control, Administration, and Maintenance of War Memorials
A
children.text: the memorial is not built on the territory of the applicable former enemy; and

children.label: B
children.text: the sponsors of the memorial consent to the Commission assuming those responsibilities and transfer to the Commission all their rights and interests in the memorial.

If reasonable effort fails to locate the sponsors of a memorial, the Commission may assume responsibility for the memorial under this subsection by agreement with the appropriate foreign authorities. A decision of the Commission to assume responsibility for a war memorial under this subsection is final.

Sponsors of a war memorial for which the Commission assumes responsibility under this subsection may transfer amounts accumulated to maintain and repair the memorial to the Commission for use in carrying out this chapter. Except as provided in subsection (c) of this section, the Commission shall deposit transferred amounts as provided in .section 2103(e) of this title

children.heading: .—Arrangements for Repair or Long-Term Maintenance of Memorials
children.label: c .—Arrangements for Repair or Long-Term Maintenance of Memorials
children.text: In assuming responsibility for a war memorial under subsection (b)(1) or (2) of this section, the Commission may arrange with the sponsors of the memorial to provide for repair or long-term maintenance of the memorial. An amount transferred to the Commission for the purpose of this subsection shall be deposited by the Commission in the fund established under subsection (d) of this section.

children.heading: .—Fund for Arrangements for Repair or Long-Term Maintenance of Memorials
children.label: d .—Fund for Arrangements for Repair or Long-Term Maintenance of Memorials
A
children.text: amounts deposited into, and interest and proceeds credited to, the fund under paragraph (2) of this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: obligations obtained under paragraph (3) of this subsection.

The Commission shall deposit into the fund the amounts that are accepted under subsection (c) of this section. The Secretary of the Treasury shall credit to the fund the interest on, and the proceeds from the sale or redemption of, obligations held in the fund.

The Secretary shall invest any part of the fund that the Commission decides is not required to meet current expenses. Each investment shall be made in an interest-bearing obligation of the United States Government, or an obligation that has its principal and interest guaranteed by the Government, that the Commission decides has a maturity suitable for the fund.

The Commission shall separately account for all amounts deposited in and expended from the fund for each war memorial for which an arrangement for repair or long-term maintenance is made under subsection (c) of this section.

children.heading: .—Demolition of War Memorial Built in a Foreign Country and Disposition of Site
children.label: e .—Demolition of War Memorial Built in a Foreign Country and Disposition of Site

children.text: the appropriate foreign authorities agree to the demolition; and

children.label: A
children.text: the sponsor of the memorial consents to the demolition; or

children.label: i
children.text: to persuade the sponsor to maintain the memorial at a standard acceptable to the Commission; or
